name: Pork Chops with Dill-Cream Gravy
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Chablis or other dry white wine
recipeInstructions:
1. Saute onions and mushrooms in butter in a large skillet until tender. Remove the skillet; set aside.
2. Sprinkle pork chops with salt and pepper; place in the skillet, and brown on both sides.
3. Combine sauteed vegetables, wine, Worcestershire sauce, and dillweed; pour over chops. Cover, reduce heat, and simmer 40 minutes or until pork chops are tender. Remove from skillet; set aside.
4. Combine water and flour in a bowl; stir until smooth. Add to pan drippings in the skillet. Cook over low heat, stirring constantly, until thickened and bubbly.
5. Stir in sour cream, and cook until thoroughly heated.
6. Serve pork chops over hot cooked rice; spoon gravy over chops, as desired.
